executive_summary: The expiration of Novo Nordisk's semaglutide patent in China triggers industry shock; expectations of rock-bottom generic drug prices impact the originator drug pricing system; Novo Nordisk loses approximately $35 billion in market value
Pfizer/Sciwind's ecnoglutide (Xianweiying) receives approval for launch; as the world's first cAMP-biased GLP-1 receptor agonist, clinical data showing 15.4% weight loss at 48 weeks garners attention
Innovent's mazdutide (Xinermei) partners with JD Health to launch offline promotional events in Sanlitun, strengthening consumer education through check-in and prize draw activities

The incident of a 19-year-old girl purchasing tirzepatide online and nearly dying from self-injection continues to escalate; illegal circulation of prescription drugs and medication safety risks become focal points on social platforms

Concerns over weight rebound after GLP-1 discontinuation grow; real-world studies show average weight rebound of only 0.5% within one year after stopping medication, but individual differences and subsequent management remain controversial
industry_overview: This week, the GLP-1 sector presents a landscape where the "patent cliff" and new drug approvals proceed in parallel. Novo Nordisk faces the impact of generic drugs resulting from the expiration of its Chinese patent, with market focus on the reshaping of the pricing system. Pfizer enters the market with the new-mechanism product Ecnoglutide, intensifying differentiated competition; Innovent actively promotes Mazdutide through offline activities. Meanwhile, warning cases of serious adverse reactions from self-purchasing and injecting via online channels have emerged across multiple platforms, reflecting dual gaps in prescription drug online sales regulation and patient education. In the overall public opinion landscape, efficacy expectations intertwine with safety concerns, while expectations of price wars coexist with competition around brand moats.

events.text: 19-year-old female experienced severe adverse reactions including nausea, vomiting, and rapid heartbeat after purchasing tirzepatide online and self-injecting; doctors warn of potential cardiac and respiratory arrest
Eli Lilly CEO annual salary of $36.70 million sparks discussion; GLP-1 dual agonist tirzepatide series annual sales approximately $36.5 billion; tirzepatide achieves 15%-20% weight loss

events.text: NMPA officially approved Ecnoglutide injection (Xianweiying) for long-term weight management in adult overweight/obese patients, as the world's first cAMP-biased GLP-1 receptor agonist
Pfizer and Sciwind reached a $495 million commercial strategic cooperation, with Pfizer obtaining exclusive commercialization rights in mainland China
Phase III clinical data: 15.4% weight loss at 48 weeks, 12.8cm waist circumference reduction, 53.1% liver fat reduction

representative_posts.summary: Semaglutide patent in China will expire in March, first batch of Chinese generics already on the way, Novo Nordisk market cap evaporated approximately $35 billion, weight-loss drugs may enter the 'cabbage price' era
Pharmacist explains four situations requiring discontinuation of long-term semaglutide use, involving serious adverse reactions and contraindications
User feedback on heart discomfort after using semaglutide, mentions price approximately 800 yuan/4 doses, warns patients with thyroid/breast nodules to use with caution
US FDA approves high-dose Wegovy HD (7.2mg), average weight loss of 20.7% in obese population, approximately 1/3 of patients lost ≥25% weight

19-year-old girl experiences nausea, vomiting, and heart discomfort after purchasing tirzepatide online for injection, doctor warns may cause cardiac and respiratory arrest, emphasizes need to follow medical advice
Analysis of Eli Lilly CEO's annual salary of $36.7 million background, tirzepatide dual-target mechanism reduces weight by 15%-20%, annual sales of $36.5 billion
JD Health holds Innovent mazdutide offline event at Sanlitun, check-in can receive movie tickets and 4980 yuan dual travel card, positioned as scientifically enjoying 'thin'
User shares using Xinerme for 17 weeks (4.5 months) lost 80 jin, no rebound after stopping medication for two months, clothing size reduced by 3 sizes
User records mazdutide first week weight change (79.7→78.8kg), feedback shows slight nausea first three days, obvious satiety later
Enotigulatide approved for market launch, as world's first cAMP-biased GLP-1 receptor agonist, 15.4% weight loss at 48 weeks, waist circumference reduced by 12.8cm
Pfizer and Sciwind reach $495 million commercialization partnership, Pfizer obtains exclusive commercialization rights in mainland China, Sciwind responsible for R&D and production
Orthopedic doctor explains secrets behind weight-loss injections, warns GLP-1 drugs not suitable for all populations
Real-world study shows, 7938 patients using semaglutide or tirzepatide had average weight rebound of only 0.5% within one year after discontinuation
